title: Betrayaswamy Temple
tagline: Betrayaswamy Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Betrayaswamy temple (also called Denkanikottai Temple) in Denkanikottai town in Krishnagiri district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu.
overview.description: Betrayaswamy temple (also called Denkanikottai Temple) in Denkanikottai town in Krishnagiri district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. The temple is Classified one among the 108 Abhimana Kshethram of Vaishnavate tradition. It is located 30 km from Hosur and 60 km from Bangalore and 318 km from Chennai, 77 km from Dharmapuri. In this temple Constructed in the Hoysala style of architecture, the temple is dedicated to Vishnu who is worshipped as Devaperumal and his consort Lakshmi as Soundaryavalli in this temple.

topPlaces.name: Denkanikottai
topPlaces.description: Denkanikottai also known as Thenkanikottai is a Panchayat Town in Krishnagiri district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. It is the headquarters of Denkanikottai Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Denkanikottai taluk
topPlaces.description: Thenkanikottai taluk, also known as Denkanikottai, is a taluk of Krishnagiri district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters is the town of Denkanikottai.

topPlaces.name: Melpuram
topPlaces.description: Melpuram is a village in Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu, India. Rubber plantation plays a role in this village's economy.

topPlaces.name: Christhupalayam
topPlaces.description: Christhupalayam, also spelled Kristhupalayam, is a village located 6 km from the major town Denkanikottai, Tamil Nadu, India. The primary occupations of the villagers are farming and labor service.

topPlaces.name: Adaikalapuram, Denkanikottai
topPlaces.description: Adaikalapuram is a village located under the administration of Mallasandiram Panchayat, Krishnagiri district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located eight kilometers away from the major town of Denkanikottai.

topPlaces.name: Thotti
topPlaces.description: Thotti is a small village in dharmapuri district, situated 80 km north-west of dharmapuri, in Tamil Nadu, India. It is located near the Cauvery River, about 20 km north-west of Musiri.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Betrayaswamy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Betrayaswamy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Betrayaswamy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (77 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (57 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Betrayaswamy Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Betrayaswamy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Betrayaswamy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Betrayaswamy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Betrayaswamy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Denkanikottai, Denkanikottai taluk, Melpuram, Christhupalayam, Adaikalapuram, Denkanikottai.
